description: Unassuming in the estimation of one's abilities or achievements; not given to showing off. 'Thasu' names the quality of modesty—presenting oneself without exaggeration or display.
sound_symbolism: The word steps back. 'Tha' /θa/ begins with gentle breath, presence understated. Then 'su' settles quietly, modesty embodied.
grammatical_notes: Functions as an adjective: 'thasu miona' (modest person). Related to 'motu' (humble) but emphasizes outward presentation rather than inner attitude toward self.

pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Modest speech avoids boasting—presenting achievements without diminishing others or inflating self.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Modest claims were trusted—communities valued those who let their work speak rather than their words.
